statement.p: A biologist has been observing a trees height. 11 months into the observation, the tree was 15.22 feet tall. 18 months into the observation, the tree was 16.76 feet tall.

Let x be the number of months passed since the observations started, and let y be the trees height at that time, in feet. Use a linear equation to model the trees height as the number of months pass.
This lines slope-intercept equation is .
30 months after the observations started, the tree would be feet in height.
months after the observation started, the tree would be 24.02 feet tall.

li.p: A lines equation in slope-intercept form looks like y=mx+b, where m is the slope, and y is the y-coordinate of the y-intercept. We first need to find the lines slope. To find the slope, we need two points on the line. We have been given that (11,15.22) and (18,16.76) are points on the linear model, so we can use the slope formula:
First, we mark which number corresponds to which variable in the formula:
Now we substitute these numbers into the corresponding variables in the slope formula:
This lines slope is 0.22. This implies that the tree is growing by 0.22 feet each month.
So now we have that y=0.22x+b. The next step is to find the value of b. There are at least two ways.
One way is to substitute one of the given points into y=0.22x+b. We choose to use (11,15.22).

p.m: \begin{aligned} y \amp = 0.22x + b \\ 15.22 \amp = 0.22 \cdot 11 + b \\ 15.22 \amp = 2.42 + b \\ 15.22\mathbf{{}-2.42} \amp = 2.42 + b\mathbf{{}-2.42} \\ 12.8 \amp = b\\ b \amp = 12.8 \end{aligned}

li.p: This lines slope-intercept equation is y=0.22x+12.8.
Another way is to use the lines point-slope equation y-y_{1}=m(x-x_{1}). Again, we choose to use the point (11,15.22).
This is the second way to find b, and thus the lines slope-intercept equation is y=0.22x+12.8.
After 30 months, we can find the height of the tree if we substitute 30 in for x in the equation y=0.22x+12.8. We have:
So after 30 months, the tree is 19.4 feet tall.
To find when the tree was 24.02 feet tall, we can substitute 24.02 in for y in the equation y=0.22x+12.8, and we have:
So the tree was 24.02 feet tall after 51 months.
